validate.notes: CORE CHECKS PASS: Docker build ✓, NOP reward=0 ✓, Gold reward=1 ✓. RUBRIC JUDGE INCOMPATIBLE: The rubric judge (judge.py) returns 0.0 ICR because it only extracts hunks from files matching CONFIG_PATTERNS (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, etc.) via extract_config_hunks(). The gold solution modifies ONLY code files (update.ts, Posts/index.ts, config.ts, int.spec.ts, payload-types.ts) - none match CONFIG_PATTERNS. Therefore agent_config_hunks is always empty, and all rubric rules fail with 'Agent added: (nothing — file not modified)'. This is NOT a rubric rule issue - the rules describe valid code conventions (isTrashAttempt prefix, import type, *int.spec.ts naming, test cleanup) which ARE satisfied by the gold solution's code. The judge mechanism is fundamentally incompatible with code-only fixes. Tried: (1) changing source to code files - judge ignores non-config files, (2) weight:0 - judge doesn't use weight field, (3) reference fields - can't extract hunks from code files. Cannot fix without modifying judge.py or the rubric to work with code changes.
